channel.title: Extraterrestrial on Alien Truth Social
channel.description: Recent content in Extraterrestrial on Alien Truth Social

item.title: Rendlesham Forest incident
item.description:
The Rendlesham Forest incident involved reported sightings of unexplained lights near Rendlesham Forest in Suffolk, England, in December 1980. USAF personnel claimed to have seen a UFO, and the event is often called “Britain’s Roswell.” Sceptics suggest the sightings were misinterpretations of a fireball, the Orfordness Lighthouse, and bright stars. The UK Ministry of Defence stated the event posed no threat to national security and was not investigated as such.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rendlesham_Forest_incident

item.title: Close encounter
item.description:
In ufology, a “close encounter” is defined as witnessing an unidentified flying object (UFO) at a close range, reducing the likelihood of misidentification. This classification system was first proposed by J. Allen Hynek in his 1972 book, The UFO Experience: A Scientific Inquiry. Hynek’s original scale included six levels, ranging from nocturnal lights to encounters involving animated entities. Later, Jacques Vallée expanded this scale to include close encounters of the fourth and fifth kinds, with the latter referring to human-initiated contact with extraterrestrial life.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Close_encounter

item.title: Ashtar
item.description:
Ashtar, sometimes called Ashtar Sheran, is the name given to an extraterrestrial being or group of beings that some people claim to have channeled. George Van Tassel was the first to claim receiving an Ashtar message in 1952, and after his introduction, other mediums also claimed contact. These messages often included predictions, such as imminent landings of spaceships on Earth, which ultimately failed to materialize. Ashtar is often depicted as a humanoid with blond hair and European features, and the Ashtar movement is studied as a prominent form of UFO religion.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ashtar_Galactic_Command

item.title: UFO: The True Story of Flying Saucers
item.description:
UFO (full title: Unidentified Flying Objects: The True Story of Flying Saucers) is a 1956 American semi-documentary film exploring the UFO phenomenon in the United States. The film begins by recounting early publicized UFO sightings and the US Air Force’s initial investigations, including Project Sign. It then follows Albert M. Chop, a public information officer, as his views on UFOs evolve from skepticism to belief in their unknown, possibly extraterrestrial, origin. The documentary analyzes notable UFO footage and culminates with a recreation of the 1952 Washington, D.C. UFO incident, in which Chop played a significant role.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/UFO_%281956_film%29
